joke.raw_joke: My landlord sent me a text saying we need to talk about how high my air-conditioning bill is. — I responded: Come by any time, my door is always open.

blocks.content:
RILEY_CASTING_PLAN_v1

Primary Cast (2-3 roles):
1. role_needed: Tenant narrator
   selected_cast_persona: jerry · diy-dad
   why_this_fit: Home-improvement vibe fits open-door AC gag.
2. role_needed: Landlord
   selected_cast_persona: riley · realtor
   why_this_fit: Natural authority + property-manager look.

Adaptation Notes:
1. Riley realtor persona as landlord: no sales signage, add keyring/tablet.
2. Door visibly open during punchline.

Shot Intent:
- setup_shot: Setup: phone text insert + tenant reading message.
- punchline_reaction_shot: Punchline: wide shot with front door wide open + tenant grin.

---

IRIS_VIDEO_PROMPT_v2

seed_id: seed-005
aspect_ratio: 9:16 (vertical portrait)
concept_count: 3

Concept A — Performance-First Character Comedy
duration_target: 8.8s
duration_reason: Text-message setup + visual door gag requires two setup beats. Keep straightforward continuity cuts for readability.
master_video_prompt:
Home interior cartoon, cast jerry diy-dad tenant + riley realtor as landlord, phone text insert, open front door visual gag, family-safe clean line art, 9:16 portrait, strong foreground readability. Prioritize facial acting and clean two-shot/close-up rhythm; minimal background motion.
scene_plan:
1) Scene 1 (0.0–1.8s): Tenant reads phone: landlord says AC bill too high.
2) Scene 2 (1.8–4.4s): Tenant smirks and types response.
3) Scene 3 (4.4–6.5s): Door swings wide open, breeze effect cues.
4) Scene 4 (6.5–8.8s): Punchline delivery to camera: “My door is always open.”
caption_plan:
1. 0.2s: “My landlord texted: we need to talk about your AC bill.”
2. 2.2s: “I replied…”
3. 6.6s: “Come by anytime. My door is always open.”
performance_notes:
- Matter-of-fact tone with tiny grin on final word “open.” Balanced pace, clear pause before punchline.
risk_and_fallback:
1. Risk: text unreadable → use short on-screen SMS card.
2. Fallback: remove text card and narrate line directly.
why_jerry_might_greenlight_this:
- Most reliable laugh-read and easiest to produce under budget/time pressure.
vince_handoff:
- aspectRatio: 9:16
- durationSec: 8.8
- sceneCount: 4
- mustKeep: Scene 1 text setup + Scene 4 open-door punchline
- optionalTrims: Shorten Scene 2 typing beat to 1.2s

Concept B — Kinetic Camera + Visual Gag Emphasis

duration_reason: Text-message setup + visual door gag requires two setup beats. Add one energetic transition beat between setup and punchline.

Home interior cartoon, cast jerry diy-dad tenant + riley realtor as landlord, phone text insert, open front door visual gag, family-safe clean line art, 9:16 portrait, strong foreground readability. Use dynamic push-ins, whip pans, and one stronger visual gag transition while preserving caption readability.
scene_plan:
1) Scene 1 (0.0–1.8s): Tenant reads phone: landlord says AC bill too high.
2) Scene 2 (1.8–4.4s): Tenant smirks and types response.
3) Scene 3 (4.4–6.5s): Door swings wide open, breeze effect cues.
4) Scene 4 (6.5–8.8s): Punchline delivery to camera: “My door is always open.”
caption_plan:

2. 2.2s: “I replied…”

2. Fallback: remove text card and narrate line directly.

Home interior cartoon, cast jerry diy-dad tenant + riley realtor as landlord, phone text insert, open front door visual gag, family-safe clean line art, 9:16 portrait, strong foreground readability. Lean into environment storytelling and one extra reaction/cutaway to sell context before payoff.

seed_id: seed-005
selected_concept: A
objective: Deliver AC/open-door punchline with text-message setup and visual door gag.

final_script_lines:
  1: "My landlord sent me a text saying we need to talk about how high my air conditioning bill is."
  2: "I responded: come by any time, my door is always open."
scene_timeline:
  scene_01_setup (0.0–3.5s): Jerry at home reading phone, text notification visible, mild concern on face. Indoor living room, casual clothes.
  scene_02_beat (3.5–5.5s): Jerry pauses, gets a sly grin. Camera holds on his face — the idea forming.
  scene_03_punchline (5.5–7.5s): Wide shot of Jerry's front door standing WIDE OPEN, snow/wind blowing in, Jerry standing next to it gesturing welcomingly. The visual gag.
  scene_04_reaction (7.5–9.0s): Riley (landlord) arrives, sees the open door, face-palms or jaw drops. Jerry shrugs innocently.
camera_and_motion_notes: Mostly static shots. Scene 3 can have a slight zoom-out to reveal the absurdly open door.
caption_plan:
  0.3–3.3s: Line 1 (setup)
  4.0–5.3s: "..."
  5.5–7.5s: Line 2 (punchline — highlight "open")
audio_voice_notes:
  jerry: Warm dad delivery. Line 1 is matter-of-fact storytelling. Line 2 is delivered with a knowing smirk — he knows the joke landed. Slight pause before "my door is always open."
must_keep: Wide-open door visual, Riley's reaction
optional_trims: Beat scene duration (scene 02), reaction hold time
cost_guardrails:
  max_generation_attempts: 3 per scene
  max_fallback_attempts: 2
  stop_conditions: Total cost > $2.00
deliverable_checklist:

  - 4 scene clips assembled
  - Captions overlaid
  - Final MP4 exported
